text.title: Jira Automation and JQL
text.summary: Automation rule patterns, JQL query cookbook, and agent-specific gotchas for operating Jira programmatically.

rule.statement: Test automation rules in a sandbox project with a copy of production schemes before promoting to production.
rule.rationale: Automation rule loops (rule A triggers rule B triggers A) are trivially created and can exhaust the monthly rule-execution quota in minutes. Sandbox testing catches cycles before they hit production.

example.code:
# Auto-assign to code reviewer on transition rule: auto_assign_reviewer trigger: issue_transitioned_to_code_review action: assign_to_field(code_reviewer)
# Close subtasks when parent is Done rule: close_subtasks trigger: parent_issue_done action: transition_subtasks_to_done
# Alert on blocker priority rule: notify_on_blocker trigger: priority_changed_to_blocker action: send_slack_message(channel=#dev-alerts)
example.note: Tag every automation-created issue with an "automation" label for audit trails.

rule.statement: Add cycle guards to every automation rule that could trigger another rule.
rule.rationale: Without cycle guards, rule A → rule B → rule A loops consume the entire monthly execution budget (Cloud: per-tier monthly limit) in seconds.

rule.statement: Always validate JQL with validateQuery=strict before executing in production.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent Jira field names. Strict validation catches hallucinated fields before they return empty result sets that silently appear as "no issues found".

example.code:
-- My open issues assignee = currentUser() AND resolution = Unresolved
-- Sprint backlog project = PROJ AND sprint in openSprints()
-- Bugs created this week project = PROJ AND type = Bug AND created >= -7d
-- Blocked items status = "In Progress" AND flagged = "Impediment"
-- Overdue issues duedate < now() AND resolution = Unresolved
-- Release scope fixVersion = "1.0.0" AND resolution = Unresolved

antipattern.description: Using JQL WAS queries on transition history for large issue sets.
antipattern.reason: WAS queries against transition history time out above ~10k issues because they scan the full changelog. Use status-based queries with date filters instead.

section.title: Agent Gotchas

rule.statement: Cache /rest/api/3/field and provide the field schema in system prompt before any JQL generation.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent field names that pass syntax validation but return empty results. The field list is stable; caching it once per session eliminates hallucination.

list.item: ADF (Atlassian Document Format) for issue descriptions != Markdown; agents writing Markdown produce empty description fields. Use the ADF conversion utility.
Permission errors return HTTP 200 with empty issue lists in some endpoints — always check the total count, not just the array.
Rate limits: Cloud allows ~50 req/sec/site; automation rules have a per-tier monthly budget. Throttle bulk operations to 5 req/sec.
Service account that creates issues becomes the reporter; disable notifications on the bot user to prevent fan-out.
Webhook signatures rotate on app reinstall; agents listening to webhooks need rotation handling.

section.reference: Push workflow JSON to sandbox project via REST API
